content.problemStatement: Write a C program to turn the string "CIVVIX" into a Palindrome. To do this, take the last character of the string and make changes to the string as needed. Finally, print the resulting palindrome.
constraints: Length of each dummy string is at most 100 characters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a dummy string which is ignored by the program.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the constant palindrome "CIVVIC" on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case; regardless of the provided string, the program outputs the palindrome "CIVVIC".
Two test cases are processed. For each, the constant palindrome "CIVVIC" is printed on its own line.
Even empty lines are accepted as dummy input; the program still prints the required palindrome three times.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
10
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
hidden.expectedOutput:
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC

solution.approach: The required output does not depend on the input strings. For each test case we simply print the fixed palindrome "CIVVIC". The algorithm reads the number of test cases, discards each dummy line and appends the constant string to the output. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space besides the output buffer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, where T is the number of test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (output buffer not counted).
